# PRD: Phase 2b — Calendar Tools + Anthropic Tool Use

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-17
**Status:** ✅ Approved by Citlali — ready for coding agent
**Requested by:** Citlali (Phase 2 continuation; foundation now encrypted)

---

## 1. Goal

Make the Executive Assistant and Events Director directors actually do calendar work. After this PRD, a user in chat with EA can say "What's on my calendar tomorrow?" or "Schedule a 30-min meeting with Z next Tuesday at 2pm" and get real answers / real calendar events. Same for Events Director on event-flavored asks.

This requires two things in one PRD:
1. **Anthropic tool-use loop in `team/[slug]/chat`** — the chat route currently does single-turn `messages.create`. We need a multi-turn loop where Claude can request tool calls, we execute them, and feed results back.
2. **Five Calendar tools** — list events, get availability, create event, update event, delete event.

## 2. Why

Phase 2a put the OAuth + token refresh foundation in place. Phase 2b makes the foundation visible to users — without tool calls, the connected Google account is invisible from the chat UX. Tool-use is also the pattern we'll reuse for Phases 2c (Gmail), 2d (Drive), 3 (Grants), and beyond. Build it once, build it well.

## 3. Architecture Decisions

- **Direct REST calls to Google Calendar v3, no `googleapis` SDK.** Tonight's Phase 2a /simplify removed `googleapis` (4MB) in favor of `google-auth-library` (50KB) + REST. Continuing that — `lib/google-calendar.ts` makes `fetch` calls to `https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/...` with a `Bearer ${accessToken}` header.
- **Tool definitions per-archetype.** `lib/tools/registry.ts` maps each archetype slug to its allowed tool set. EA + Events Director both get the full Calendar suite tonight. Other archetypes get an empty set (they'll get their own tools in 2c, 2d, 3, etc.). Slugs that aren't in the registry get no tools — no hidden cross-contamination.
- **Tool-use loop with safety cap.** The chat route loops up to 8 tool-call rounds before hard-stopping. Real conversations rarely exceed 2-3; 8 is a guardrail against runaway loops.
- **Tools return JSON strings to Claude, errors as content (not exceptions).** When a tool fails (Google not connected, API 403, etc.), the route adds a tool_result with `is_error: true` and a human-readable message, instead of throwing. Claude can then explain the failure to the user. This is the recommended Anthropic pattern.
- **Calendar IDs.** Most operations target the user's "primary" calendar by default. Tools accept an optional `calendarId` parameter; when omitted, defaults to `'primary'` (Google's special alias for the user's main calendar).
- **Time zone handling.** All tool inputs/outputs use ISO 8601 timestamps with explicit timezone offsets. Claude is instructed (via system prompt addition) to ALWAYS interpret user-stated times in the user's timezone if known. We don't do timezone inference in code — it's the model's job. The tool just respects whatever ISO it's given.

## 4. Scope

### In scope

1. **`apps/web/src/lib/google-calendar.ts`** (NEW) — typed wrappers around 5 Google Calendar v3 REST endpoints:
   - `listEvents({ accessToken, calendarId?, timeMin?, timeMax?, maxResults? })` → `{ events: CalendarEvent[] }` (default `maxResults: 25`)
   - `getEvent({ accessToken, calendarId?, eventId })` → `{ event: CalendarEvent }`
   - `createEvent({ accessToken, calendarId?, summary, start, end, description?, attendees?, location? })` → `{ event: CalendarEvent }`
   - `updateEvent({ accessToken, calendarId?, eventId, ...patchFields })` → `{ event: CalendarEvent }`
   - `deleteEvent({ accessToken, calendarId?, eventId })` → `{ success: boolean }`
   - All functions throw a `GoogleCalendarError` (custom class) on non-2xx with status code + message
   - Type definition for `CalendarEvent` matching the subset we surface to Claude

2. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/calendar.ts`** (NEW) — Anthropic tool definitions + handler dispatch:
   - 5 `Anthropic.Tool` objects with `name`, `description`, `input_schema`
   - `executeCalendarTool({ name, input, accessToken })` async function — switches on name, calls the right `google-calendar.ts` function, returns either `{ content: string }` or `{ content: string, is_error: true }`
   - Tool names: `calendar_list_events`, `calendar_get_event`, `calendar_create_event`, `calendar_update_event`, `calendar_delete_event`
   - Descriptions are MODEL-FACING — write them carefully; Claude uses these to decide when to call which tool

3. **`apps/web/src/lib/tools/registry.ts`** (NEW) — central tool dispatch:
   ```ts
   export const ARCHETYPE_TOOLS: Record<ArchetypeSlug, Anthropic.Tool[]> = {
     'executive-assistant': calendarTools,
     'events-director': calendarTools,
     'development-director': [],
     'marketing-director': [],
     'programs-director': [],
     'hr-volunteer-coordinator': [],
   };
   
   export async function executeTool({ name, input, orgId, serviceClient }): Promise<{ content: string; is_error?: boolean }> {
     // dispatch on name prefix: 'calendar_' → executeCalendarTool, etc.
     // gets the access token via getValidGoogleAccessToken
     // returns { content: "Google Workspace not connected. Ask the user to visit /dashboard/integrations." , is_error: true } when not connected
   }
   ```

4. **Refactor `apps/web/src/app/api/team/[slug]/chat/route.ts`** — add tool-use loop:
   - Look up `tools = ARCHETYPE_TOOLS[slug]`
   - First `messages.create` call includes `tools` array (and `tool_choice: { type: 'auto' }` is the default)
   - In a loop (max 8 iterations):
     - If response `stop_reason === 'tool_use'`: extract every `tool_use` block from `response.content`, call `executeTool` for each (in parallel via `Promise.all`), build `tool_result` content blocks, append assistant + user turns to messages, call `messages.create` again
     - If `stop_reason === 'end_turn'`: break, save the final assistant text + return to client
     - Any other stop_reason: break, return what we have
   - On loop cap hit (8 rounds): append a synthetic assistant message ("I've used too many tools — let me try a simpler approach.") and return
   - **Persistence:** save BOTH the user's original message AND the final assistant text (with all intermediate tool calls) to the `messages` table. Tool-call rounds in the middle are NOT persisted as separate rows — the user only sees the user→assistant exchange. The full transcript including tool calls can be reconstructed for debugging from server logs.

5. **System prompt addition** — for archetypes with tools, append a brief instruction to the existing system prompt:
   ```
   You have access to the user's Google Calendar via tools. Always use them when the user asks about calendar events, scheduling, or availability. Never make up event details — if you don't know, call list_events or get_event. Times in your responses should match the timezone the user used in their question.
   ```
   This goes into the existing prompt-building logic (find where the archetype prompt is constructed; append the addendum when tools are present).

6. **Error handling — Google not connected**
   - When `getValidGoogleAccessToken` returns `{ error: NextResponse }` (integration missing), `executeTool` returns `{ content: "Google Workspace is not connected for this organization. Please visit Settings → Integrations to connect a Google account.", is_error: true }`
   - Claude will then explain this to the user instead of pretending to look at a calendar

7. **Replace the test-calendars endpoint?** Leave it alone — it's harmless. Add a comment noting the real Calendar functionality is now in tool form, and the test endpoint exists for diagnostic use only.

### Out of scope (Phase 2c, 2d, 3, etc.)

- Gmail tools (Phase 2c)
- Drive tools (Phase 2d)
- Grant tools (Phase 3)
- Any non-EA, non-Events archetype getting Calendar tools
- Recurring event support (separate scope — Google's recurrence syntax is non-trivial)
- Free/busy queries across multiple calendars at once (separate)
- Calendar sharing / ACL operations
- Attachments
- Webhook subscriptions (push notifications for changes)
- UI changes to the chat to show "called calendar_list_events" — Claude already mentions tool use naturally in its responses

## 5. Acceptance Criteria

Done when:
1. ✅ A user with Google connected can chat with the Executive Assistant and ask "What's on my calendar tomorrow?" — gets a real list of events
2. ✅ The user can ask "Schedule a meeting with Z tomorrow at 2pm called 'PRD review'" — a real event is created on their primary calendar
3. ✅ A user WITHOUT Google connected gets a friendly "please connect Google in Settings" message from the EA when asking calendar questions, NOT a fake calendar
4. ✅ Other archetypes (Marketing, Dev, Programs, HR) do NOT have access to calendar tools — verifiable by reading the registry
5. ✅ Tool-use loop terminates correctly on `end_turn`; loop cap of 8 rounds is enforced
6. ✅ Tool errors (Google API 403, network failure) are surfaced as `is_error: true` tool results, NOT as 5xx responses
7. ✅ `npm run build` passes
8. ✅ Single commit on main, message starting `feat: Calendar tools + Anthropic tool-use loop (Phase 2b)`

## 6. Risks & Open Questions

- **Token refresh during a tool-use loop.** If a request takes long enough that the access token expires mid-loop (rare but possible), `getValidGoogleAccessToken` is called fresh per tool call, so refresh happens transparently. The `refreshInFlight` Map dedups concurrent refreshes within a request (parallel tool calls in the same round).
- **Google API rate limits.** Calendar v3 free quota is 1M queries/day, 600 queries/100s/user. We're nowhere near hitting these for MVP. No client-side backoff for now.
- **Cost.** Each tool-use round = one Claude turn. A 3-round conversation = 3 Claude calls instead of 1. Roughly triples per-conversation cost for tool-using archetypes. Acceptable for MVP.
- **OAuth client still in Testing mode.** Same caveat as Phase 2a: only test users on the consent screen allowlist can authorize, so end-to-end Calendar functionality only works for `edifysaas@gmail.com` and any other listed test users until Z initiates Google verification.
- **`getValidGoogleAccessToken` signature.** Currently returns `{ accessToken } | { error: NextResponse }`. The error path is awkward when called from `executeTool` (which doesn't return a NextResponse — it returns a tool result). The agent should refactor `executeTool` to translate the NextResponse error into a string-form tool error, OR add a second variant of the helper that returns `{ accessToken } | { reason: string }`. Up to the agent — both are fine.

## 7. Estimated Effort

~30 min agent + ~10 min /simplify pass.

- [x] Citlali approved (2026-04-17 ~11:01 PM EDT, with the "do all three" decision)
- [x] Architecture pre-locked
